title: pouteria minima
intro: Pouteria minima, also known as the Brazilian cherry or wild cherry, is a small, deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The bark and leaves of Pouteria minima contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been shown to have health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections. In traditional medicine, Pouteria minima is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its herbal preparations are also used in folk remedies to treat various ailments.
